name: Marketing Performance Review
description: Quarterly review of marketing's contribution to pipeline and revenue, written defensibly for CFO and CEO audiences. Replaces 2–3 days of manual board-prep work per cycle.
alsoSurfaces: What did marketing produce this quarter — pipeline, revenue, efficiency?
Which channels grew, which declined, and by how much?
Where should we scale spend next quarter, and where should we cut?
What's the one-line summary I can defend in front of the CFO?
beforeWeRun: Time window — last completed fiscal quarter, or custom?
Comparison period — prior quarter, or year-ago quarter?
Which metrics — sourced pipeline, sourced revenue, CAC, ROAS, channel breakdown (or all)?

whatYoullGet.name: Executive summary
whatYoullGet.desc: Three to five sentences with sourced pipeline, sourced revenue, period-over-period direction, and the single most important takeaway.

whatYoullGet.name: Quarter highlights
whatYoullGet.desc: Bulleted list of 4–6 specific developments — channels that grew, channels that declined, efficiency changes, notable campaigns.

whatYoullGet.name: Channel performance
whatYoullGet.desc: Sortable table with spend, sourced pipeline, revenue, CAC, ROAS, and PoP deltas per channel.

whatYoullGet.name: Investment efficiency
whatYoullGet.desc: Two-paragraph narrative on CAC and ROAS trends, with explicit over/under-investment calls.

whatYoullGet.name: Next quarter recommendation
whatYoullGet.desc: Forward-looking — channels to scale, channels to reduce, and one open question for leadership.

whatYoullGet.name: Sources & definitions
whatYoullGet.desc: Canonical model, every source read with row counts, channel mapping, and any data quality flags.

callout: 5 quick inputs, then Sage drafts your performance review in ~15 min.
